revision.text:
{{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | Image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997年3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|ja|紀貫之}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/townguide/temp/otomojinja.html Ōtomo Shrine] - Saku City Official Site * [https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2048.htm Ōtomo Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association

{{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | Image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997年3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|ja|紀貫之}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

{{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | Image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997年3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|ja|紀貫之}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

{{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | Image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997年3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|ja|紀貫之}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997年3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|ja|紀貫之}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997年3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|ja|紀貫之}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

{{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | Image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997年3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|ja|紀貫之}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}

'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997年3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|ja|紀貫之}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997年3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|ja|紀貫之}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}.
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997年3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|ja|紀貫之}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432350]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|qq=|1=Amanomichine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940767}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Takehi|qq=}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997th year 3月）</ref>.
During the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by [[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]]: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/townguide/temp/otomojinja.html Ōtomo Shrine] - Saku City Official Site * [https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2048.htm Ōtomo Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432350]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|1=Amanomichine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940767}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Takehi|qq=}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997th year 3月）</ref>.
During the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by [[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]]: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/townguide/temp/otomojinja.html Ōtomo Shrine] - Saku City Official Site * [https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/02kitasaku/2048.htm Ōtomo Shrine] - Nagano Shrine Association

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432350]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|1=Amanomichine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940767}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Takehi|qq=}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997th year 3月）</ref>.
During the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by [[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]]: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Takehi|qq=}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997th year 3月）</ref>.
During the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by [[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]]: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432350]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|1=Amanomichine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940767}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Takehi|qq=}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997th year 3月）</ref>.
During the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by [[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]]: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432350]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|1=Amanomichine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940767}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Takehi|qq=}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997th year 3月）</ref>.
During the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by [[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]]: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432350]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|1=Amanomichine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940767}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Takehi|qq=}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997th year 3月）</ref>.
During the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by [[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]]: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432350]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|1=Amanomichine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940767}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]].
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Takehi|qq=}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997th year 3月）</ref>.
During the [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]], were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by [[:en:Ki no Tsurayuki|Ki no Tsurayuki]]: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432350]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|1=Amanomichine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940767}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}} | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}.
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Takehi|qq=}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997th year 3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō era}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|en|Ki no Tsurayuki|WD=Q468288|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴神社 (佐久市望月)]]
{{Shrine in Japan | Name = Ōtomo Shrine | image = [[File:大伴神社拝殿.jpg|250px]] | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Saku, Nagano|Saku]] City, Mochizuki, Ōkiriya 227 | Latitude = 36|15|59.8 | Longitude = 138|21|36.5 | ISO = JP-20 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Nagano | Deities = {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}, {{ill|Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|ja|天道根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukune-no-Mikoto|1=Amanomichine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940767}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} | Foundation = Traditional: 40th year of Emperor Keikō | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | Pilgrimage Sites = | Chief Priest = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Ōtomo Shrine''' (大伴神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku, Nagano|1=Saku, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture. The shrine is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}.
== Overview == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō’s reign when {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Otomo no Takehiko|lt=Ōtomo Takehi|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Takehi|qq=}} (now worshiped as {{ill|Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}}) descended to the area on horseback and enshrined himself there<ref name="#1">式内社研究会『式内社調査報告書 第十三・十四巻』（皇学館大学出版部）</ref>.
Another legend tells of {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} riding a dragon horse across the great ocean, surveying the rivers and valleys, ascending the Chikuma River, and enshrining himself at this location<ref>北條勝貴「松尾大社における市杵嶋姫命の鎮座について」『国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第72集』（1997th year 3月）</ref>.
During the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō era|1=Taiho}} (701–704), imperial pastures (官牧) were established across the provinces. A plateau, 700 to 800 meters high, bordered by the Chikuma and Kakuma Rivers, was designated as a royal pasture directly managed by the court, becoming known as "Mochizuki Pasture" (望月牧). The local landholders, identified as descendants of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}, were appointed as caretakers of the pastures. The Mochizuki clan, descended from the Ōtomo, also managed nearby Nagakura and Shiono Pastures.
The fame of Mochizuki's horses extended to the capital, inspiring a poem by {{ill|Ki no Tsurayuki|en|Ki no Tsurayuki|WD=Q468288|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}}: *"At the pure waters of Ōsaka Barrier, I see shadows—surely Mochizuki's steed is being led away."*<ref name="#1"/>.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
